Great insights on $MELI.

Here are three things I am really excited for the thesis. 

The data flywheel: merging marketplace purchase history with off-network Mercado Pago transactions creates an unparalleled credit underwriting advantage in a region historically plagued by weak credit bureaus.

The cost of funding catalyst: the recent regulatory applications with Mexico's CNBV and the Central Bank of Argentina, pending banking licenses will soon unlock cheap retail deposits. This shift structurally lowers capital costs and permanently boosts an already massive 20%+ NIMAL.

Finally, the advertising walled garden: according to eMarketer and Harding Loevner data, Mercado Ads now controls nearly half of Latin America's digital retail media spend at roughly ~80% margins. This high-profit, closed-loop ad network (that incorporates data from both fintech and commerce) directly subsidizes the aggressive free-shipping war against competitors, making MercadoLibre's long-term dominance incredibly difficult to disrupt.

📱 Se recalienta la pelea entre billeteras digitales.

5/ That matters because better data becomes better underwriting.

MercadoPago’s credit revenues grew from $246M in 2020 to $5.9B in 2025, while its loan book reached $12.5B.

The model mirrors banking-first neobanks: payments may be the wedge, but lending becomes the engine. https://t.co/FvErgHece0

4/ Superapps show a different path to the same destination.

MercadoPago, Grab, and WeChat did not start with banking. They started with commerce, mobility, or messaging, then embedded financial services into an existing daily-use relationship.

The moat was not the card. It was the data.

TL:DR - Merge is a worthwhile event to attend, happy to be back in the conference era, Latam builders have unmatched energy 

general takes:

- neobanks, payment apps, yield products. if you skipped the lead gen, check below (link in the comments)

- the ecosystem is big, but very well-connected. s/o to @AriEiberman, @cryptochica_arg , @cioffi_victor, and others if you want to know what's happening in the region

- the main venue reminded me a lot of my non-crypto tech events. side events were the quality of a global crypto week. kudos to @SuperteamBR and @PodsFinance for being universally the best hosts

- institutions and crypto can be friends in two cases: 1) survival and coexisting (neobanks); 2) when it's a numbers game: either you scale with the bank/corpo leverage, or become big enough for them to notice
not. everyone. needs. institutional arm.

- we should have more events in Latam, and I'm not biased! not a single complaint about the market from these people

thinking my mind loud:

- crypto is inevitable in Latam. when everything is KYC (even ordering food), and you can't trust banks for tomorrow, onchain is the answer

- PIX is a good system + Mercado apps, but this works more for the younger generation

- the US and european arms will pour even more money into the region, because they see a huge userbase and presume adoption is here. whatever is good for the local economy, but I believe many operators will enter the market with pretty aggressive expansion

- local consumer trend in crypto is mobile payments and long-term investments (low-risk DeFi). the latter can be unfortunately extractive

- it's a great market to experiment with, but you should know the language and offer a very simple, basic product to begin with

was beyond happy to meet many @sideshiftai users and confirm we're doing the right things
